LAW AND MOTION 301, RE: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT PAUL DIMAURO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO DEPT. 301 A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/HEK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Continued To Jul-01-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PAUL DIMAURO's, AN INDIVIDUAL, MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Paul DiMauro's motion to quash service of summons and to dismiss for lack of personal jurisdiction and forum non conveniens is denied in its entirety.

Mr. DiMauro's solicitation of a California resident to participate in his investments in securities constitutes "purposeful availment of forum benefits" and thus is sufficient to impose specific personal jurisdiction over him in a case where plaintiff's claim is based on his participation in those investments. This is true even though Mr. DiMauro's contacts were only via text messages. Zehia v. Superior Court (2020) 45 Cal. App. 5th 543, Moncrief v. Clark (2015) 238 Cal. App. 4th 1000, and Hall v. LaRonde (1997) 56 Cal. App. 4th 1342 support specific jurisdiction over Mr. DiMauro. Nor has Mr. DiMauro presented a "compelling case" that requiring him to defend this case in California is unfair or unreasonable. (Moncrief, 238 Cal. App. 4th at 1008).

Mr. DiMauro has not satisfied his burden of showing that this court is an inconvenient forum. While Mr. Lamy no longer resides in California, his choice of a California forum is entitled to "due deference." (National Football League v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2013) 216 Cal. App. 4th 902, 929-930). Notably Mr. DiMauro does not address the balance of private and public interest factors , which is part of the burden he bears on this motion. (Morris v. AGFA Corp. (2006) 144 Cal. App. 4th 1452, 1463). Given the partial overlap of this case with the San Francisco venued marital dissolution case between Mr. Lamy and Mr. DiMauro's daughter, there is a significant public interest for this case to remain in this court. (See, e.g., Klein v. Superior Court (1988) 198 Cal. App. 3d 894, 901 (significant weight given to location of related litigation)). Moreover, relatively speaking, this is a simple case that would have at most a de minimis impact on court congestion. (Roulier v. Cannondale (2002) 101 Cal. App. 4th 1180. 1189-1190).

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)
